DECLARATION OF GREGORY WINSLOW IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITIONS; MOTION TO COMPEL MEDICAL EXAMINATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF MEDWADOWSKI, BARBARA

DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: MICHAEL MCCONATHY, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANTS/MOVING PARTIES, M. BURKE ENTERPRISES, INC., CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. GREGORY F. WINSLOW, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY. HEARING HELD. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DID NOT SEEK ITS OWN IME IN A TIMELY FASHION, HAVING APPARENTLY RELIED UPON DEFENDANT M. BURKE ENTERPRISES, INC.'S NOTICE OF INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION. THE DEFENDANTS ARE REPRESENTED BY THE SAME COUNSEL, AND COUNSEL SIGNED THE NOTICE AS COUNSEL FOR BOTH DEFENDANTS. THE EXAMINATION WAS UNDERTAKEN UPON AN UNDERSTANDING THAT DR. FALCES WOULD CONDUCT THE EXAMINATION OF THE VARIOUS ASPECTS OF PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES AND PRESENT PROGNOSIS, INCLUDING THE SHOULDER INJURY. HAVING MADE THAT STRATEGIC DECISION, DEFENDANTS CANNOT NOW DEMAND AN EXAMINATION TO TAKE PLACE LESS THAN 30 DAYS PRIOR TO TRIAL. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
REPLY BRIEF IN SUPP OF MTN TO BIFURCATE LIABILTY FROM DAMAGES AT TRIAL; DECL OF MCCONATHY FILED BY DEFTS
